name: reasoning-boundaries-test-time
description: Enhances LLM specification alignment through test-time deliberation that reasons across task boundaries, improving adherence to complex requirements. Use during inference to improve model adherence to specifications through deliberative reasoning process.
paperTitle: Reasoning over Boundaries: Enhancing Specification Alignment via Test-time Deliberation
content:
# Reasoning over Boundaries: Enhancing Specification Alignment via Test-time Deliberation

Extracted from [arXiv:2509.14760](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.14760)

## Core Concept

This paper introduces techniques for enhancing specification alignment via test-time deliberation. The work addresses key challenges in making reasoning more practical and scalable for real-world applications.

```
│  - Core Algorithm/Method                │
│  - Optimization                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Output: Result/Optimization            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Practical Guidance

### When to Use
- Use during inference to improve model adherence to specifications through deliberative reasoning process.
- When optimizing for specification-alignment
- In scenarios requiring test-time

### When NOT to Use
- When simpler baselines suffice
- Without adequate computational resources
- For domains outside the paper's scope

### Common Pitfalls
- Not properly tuning hyperparameters
- Insufficient data for effective training
- Ignoring domain-specific constraints

### Hyperparameters

| Parameter | Default | Range | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-------|-------|
| `learning_rate` | 1e-4 | 1e-5 to 1e-2 | Adjust based on convergence |
| `batch_size` | 32 | 8 to 256 | Depends on GPU memory |
| `num_epochs` | 10 | 1 to 100 | More epochs = more training |
